q: Fluid is a substance that offers no permanent resistance to ___ forces.
Fluid statics concentrates on fluids at ___.
Fluid dynamics concerns with the fluid in ___.
The study of the flow of liquid is called ___.
Hydrostatic is the study of liquid at ___.
Surface tension and capillarity, which are important concepts of hydrostatic, are included at the ___ of the chapter.
The volume of a gas depends on pressure and temperature obeying Boyle's law and ___ law.
Hence, the liquid is incompressible (i.e. ___ of liquid is constant for any pressure provided that the temperature is kept constant).
In the flow of fluids, the path or flow of the fluid particles is called ___.
Motion of fluids can be classified by two types, laminar flow and ___ flow.
In steady laminar flow, streamlines do not ___ each other.
In turbulent flow the speed of the fluid at a point is continuously undergoing changes in both ___ and direction.
Ideal laminar fluid flow considers that the fluid is ___.
Internal friction in fluids is called ___.
For example, sea breeze is a laminar flow and storm is a ___ flow.
The mass flowing in section 1 must equal the mass flowing in section 2. Hence, volume flow rate $\frac{V}{t}$ is the ___ throughout the pipe.
The equation $A_1 v_1 = A_2 v_2$ states that the liquid entering one end of the pipe leaves from the other end with a constant ___ flow rate.
The larger the area of the pipe, the ___ is the speed.
The speed of water spraying from a hose increases by making the hose area ___.
Bernoulli's equation, in fluid dynamics, relates the pressure, flow speed, and ___ for the steady (laminar) flow of an ideal incompressible fluid.
Bernoulli's equation can be derived using the ___ principle.
According to work-energy principle, the work done on this section of fluid must equal the change in total ___ energy.

In words, Bernoulli's theorem states that the sum of pressure energy, kinetic energy and potential energy per unit volume of an incompressible, non-viscous fluid in a streamlined laminar flow remains ___.
The air flow moving above the top curved surface of the wing has a longer distance to travel and needs to go ___ to have the same transit time.
High velocity of air flow is high along the top surface and low along the bottom surface. This causes a ___ pressure above the wing.
When the piston of the sprayer is pressed, the fast moving air causes a low pressure at the tip of the ___.
The liquid inside the container is at a higher pressure, and therefore rushes up through the ___ and out the nozzle.
The ___ effect is a particular manifestation of Bernoulli's theorem that is commonly associated with a spinning object moving through a fluid.
During cyclones, moving air gain speed above the roof of a house causing the ___ pressure on the roof.
Most of the fluids are not ideal ones and offer some ___ to motion.
The resistance to fluid motion (or) internal friction of fluid is called ___.
Viscosity arises when there is ___ motion between layers of the fluid.
A fluid with large viscosity resists motion because its strong ___ forces give it a lot of internal friction.
The viscosity of liquids decreases rapidly with an increase in ___.
The viscosity of gases increases with an increase in ___.
Viscosity does not change as the amount of matter changes, therefore it is an ___ property.
The SI unit of viscosity is ___ second ($\text{Pa}\cdot\text{s}$).
Pascal second is equivalent to ___ second per metre squared ($\text{N}\cdot\text{s}\cdot\text{m}^{-2}$).
The SI unit of viscosity is sometimes referred to as the ___ ($\text{Pl}$).
One way is to measure fluid's resistance to flow when an external force is applied. This is known as ___ viscosity.
The other way is to measure the resistive flow of a fluid under the weight of gravity. It is known as ___ viscosity.
Newton's law of viscosity states that the shear stress between the two adjacent layers of fluid is directly proportional to the negative value of the ___ gradient.
Shear stress is the shearing force per unit ___.
Viscosity is defined as the ratio of shearing stress to the ___ gradient.
In symbols, shear stress $\tau \propto \frac{dv}{dy}$ (or) $\tau = -\eta ___$.
In the equation $\tau = -\eta \frac{dv}{dy}$, $\eta$ is the viscosity (___ of viscosity).
In Figure 3.8, $\Delta v = v_1 - v_2$, $\Delta y \rightarrow 0$, and $\frac{\Delta v}{\Delta y} = ___$.
Shear stress is the shearing force per unit _.
In the formulation of shear stress, the expression $\frac{dv}{dy}$ is the _.
Viscosity is defined as the ratio of shearing stress to the _.
Stokes' law states that the drag force $F$ acting upward in resistance to the fall of a spherical object in a viscous liquid is $F = $ _.
A simple way of measuring viscosity is that a small sphere ball is dropped through the fluid and time of fall and _ of fall are measured.
From the distance and time of fall, the _ can be determined.
The upward thrust acting on a sphere ball inside a fluid is given verbatim by $F_1 = $ _.
At a constant velocity of fall (terminal velocity), the upward and downward forces are in _.
The resistance to fluid motion (or) internal resistance of fluid is called _.
Surface tension is the tendency of the surface of a liquid to behave like a stretched _ skin.
Cohesion refers to forces between molecules of the _ substance.
Adhesion refers to forces between molecules of _ substances.
Surface tension is defined as the force per unit _ that acts across any line on a surface tending to pull the surface apart.
The SI unit of surface tension is _.
Surface tension can also be referred to as a force because it is the tension between _ molecules.
Capillary action is defined as the _ flow of a liquid into a narrow tube or porous material.
Capillary action is caused by the combination of cohesive forces of the liquid and the _ forces between the liquid and tube material.
In order for wicking to occur, a tube needs to be sufficiently _ in diameter.
The smaller the bore, the _ the water will rise as shown in Figure 3.10 (a).
Liquids that rise in small bore tubes inserted into the liquid are said to _ the tube.
Liquids that are depressed within thin tubes below the surface of the surrounding liquid do not _ the tube.
Water level rises until there is a sufficient mass of liquid for _ forces to overcome these intermolecular forces.
The height $h$ to which the liquid can be lifted is given by $h = $ _.
If fluid particles are moving steady in smooth paths in layers, with each layer moving smoothly past the adjacent layers with no mixing; such a flow is called _ flow.
If the flow or path of the fluid particles are irregular, their direction is always changing or whirling; this fluid movement is called _ flow.

Bernoulli's theorem states that the sum of pressure, kinetic energy per unit volume and potential energy per unit volume of an incompressible, non-viscous fluid in a streamlined laminar flow remains _.
Since the circular plate has only one _ in contact with the liquid surface, $\gamma = \frac{F}{l}$.
Since the needle has two _ in contact with the liquid surface, $\gamma = \frac{F}{2l}$.
Capillarity (or) capillary action is also the rise or _ of a liquid in a narrow tube of small cross-sectional area.
A horizontal circular loop of wire has two rims (_ and inner rims) in contact with the liquid surface.
